commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=11a832b26dfa1c0ded44ad8593fa2eb0fa0ab6fd)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `memory search --json` to await short-term recall recording before writing JSON, and updates the CLI regression test to assert the recall entry is present immediately after the JSON search command returns.

PR surface: Source +5, Tests -15. Total -10 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect at source level: the new JSON branch awaits recall recording before `writeJson`, and the recall lock can wait up to 10 seconds. The linked QMD exit issue still needs real or faithful stubbed process-lifecycle proof after the patch.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer follow-up because the branch has a blocking code finding and the required real QMD behavior proof cannot be supplied by a ClawSweeper repair marker on the contributor's behalf.

target.title: fix(memory-core): settle json search cleanup
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91821.

`openclaw memory search <query> --json` could print valid JSON while best-effort short-term recall recording was still running in the background. With QMD-backed results this matches the reported shape: QMD returns results, OpenClaw writes JSON, but the Node process can stay alive on recall/SQLite/lock handles.

This keeps the fix narrow to the successful JSON search path: JSON mode now settles the existing short-term recall cleanup point before writing JSON and returning. Non-JSON search keeps the prior best-effort recall behavior.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts` — 68 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts` — 123 passed
- `pnpm format:check extensions/memory-core/src/cli.runtime.ts extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts` — clean
- `git diff --check` — clean
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` — completed after the fresh worktree lacked `node_modules`
- `git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD` — `11a832b26d Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com> fix(memory-core): settle json search recall cleanup`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: successful `memory search --json` no longer returns from the CLI runtime while short-term recall recording is still outstanding after the JSON result path.

Real environment tested: macOS local source worktree, Node 24.15.0, fresh isolated git worktree at `upstream/main`, memory-core CLI runtime test harness with dreaming enabled and a memory search hit that triggers short-term recall recording.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: the updated regression `settles short-term recall entries before completing json memory search` calls `runMemoryCli(["search", "glacier", "--json"])` and immediately reads the short-term recall store without polling. It passes only if JSON search completion waits for the recall side effect to settle.

Observed result after fix: `extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts` passed 68 tests, and `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts` passed 123 tests. The QMD manager suite also confirms one-shot CLI mode does not start watchers or background update timers.

What was not tested: a live installed `qmd` binary. I attempted a stubbed end-to-end `openclaw memory search glacier --json` run with `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and a fake QMD command, but this local built checkout did not expose the `memory` plugin CLI command through the command registry, so the command could not reach the QMD path. The runtime-level regression covers the process-lifecycle bug shape directly.
target.author: turbotheturtle
